commit.message:
docs(plugin): document direct Copilot install

Add the direct `copilot plugin install manmartgarc/negent:plugin` flow alongside the marketplace install steps and link the GitHub Copilot CLI plugin docs.

This gives Copilot users a copy-pasteable install command when they do not want to register the marketplace first, and keeps the CLI `auto` guidance aligned with GitHub's documented `OWNER/REPO:PATH` install syntax:
https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/customize-copilot/plugins-finding-installing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Manuel Martinez <manmartgarc@gmail.com>

commit.message:
fix: GUI service X11 auth — add XAUTHORITY + xhost for display access

systemctl start agrikd-gui and desktop shortcut failed silently because:
1. XAUTHORITY env var was missing — X11 server rejected connections
2. xhost didn't grant access to the agrikd service user

Now setup_jetson.sh:
- Auto-detects XAUTHORITY path (gdm, home dir variants)
- Runs xhost +SI:localuser:agrikd for X11 access
- Sets QT_QPA_PLATFORM=xcb for PyQt5
- Adds /run/user to ReadWritePaths for X11 socket access

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: auto-resolve CWD to install root for engine path resolution

Both gui_app.py and main.py now auto-detect the install root on startup:
- Host layout: /opt/agrikd/app/main.py → CWD = /opt/agrikd/
- Docker layout: /app/main.py → CWD = /app/

This ensures relative paths in config.json (models/*, config/*, data/*)
resolve correctly regardless of where the script is launched from.

Previously, running 'python3 /opt/agrikd/app/gui_app.py' from any
directory other than /opt/agrikd/ would fail to find engine files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Task 3: Lighting compositing pass

Wire up light data to shaders so lights affect the scene visually:

- Add m_worldPass flag to GLRenderer to differentiate world vs UI rendering
- beginFrame() sets m_worldPass=true, endFrame() sets it false
- uploadLightUniforms() sends ambient=(1,1,1) and zero lights when
  m_worldPass is false, ensuring UI renders at full brightness
- Add test player point light (warm white, radius 300, intensity 0.6)
  in main.cpp that follows the player position
- Player light added in both main render path and transition render path
- Update architecture.md, move completed task to CompletedTasks.md

Wired into the API project:
- EmployeeDbContext registered on same warpdb connection string

- Employee endpoints mapped in Program.cs
- Project added to solution file

commit.message:
fix(#195): switch to PendingIntent OAuth delivery for Samsung compatibility (#198)

* fix(#195): switch to PendingIntent OAuth delivery for Samsung compatibility

Replace ActivityResultLauncher-based OAuth flow with AppAuth's
performAuthorizationRequest() + PendingIntent delivery.

Samsung's aggressive memory management on S23 Ultra (Android 16) kills
AppAuth's AuthorizationManagementActivity while the Chrome Custom Tab is
open, causing the ActivityResultLauncher callback to never fire and leaving
the user stuck on the sign-in screen.

Changes:
- HuggingFaceAuthRepository: replace buildAuthIntent() with startAuthFlow()
  and add deliverAuthResponse(intent) + keep handleAuthResponse(intent)
- HuggingFaceAuthManager: implement startAuthFlow() using
  performAuthorizationRequest() with PendingIntents pointing to MainActivity;
  implement deliverAuthResponse() dispatching token exchange on IO
- AndroidManifest.xml: add android:launchMode="singleTop" to MainActivity
  so onNewIntent() is called on the existing instance instead of re-creating
- MainActivity: inject HuggingFaceAuthRepository; override onNewIntent() to
  detect AppAuth response extras and call deliverAuthResponse()
- OnboardingViewModel/Screen: replace buildAuthIntent()+handleAuthResponse()
  with startAuth(); remove ActivityResultLauncher wiring
- SettingsViewModel/Screen: same replacement as Onboarding

The isAuthenticated StateFlow is updated by deliverAuthResponse() on success,
so the Onboarding and Settings screens react automatically via their existing
Flow collection — no additional wiring needed.

Closes #195

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): authResult SharedFlow for UI feedback + Mutex for sign-out race

- Add authResult: SharedFlow<Result<Unit>> to HuggingFaceAuthRepository interface
- Implement _authResult MutableSharedFlow in HuggingFaceAuthManager; emit from deliverAuthResponse()
- Replace ad-hoc CoroutineScope() calls with class-level managerScope (SupervisorJob + IO)
- Add authMutex (Mutex) to serialise handleAuthResponse state-write and signOut
- Restructure handleAuthResponse: token exchange in suspendCancellableCoroutine stage 1,
  persist + state-update under authMutex.withLock in stage 2
- Collect authRepository.authResult in OnboardingViewModel.init → emit AuthSuccess/AuthError events
- Collect authRepository.authResult in SettingsViewModel.init → emit saveSuccess/saveError
- Fix pre-existing OnboardingScreen.kt: uiState.downloadState → uiState.gemmaDownloadState

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): move sign-out log inside mutex lock

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: retrigger CI for ae6cfda

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#195): update OnboardingScreen to use startAuth() PendingIntent API

Remove authLauncher (old Intent-based flow) and update onSignIn
callback to call viewModel.startAuth() instead. Auth result is now
delivered via MainActivity.onNewIntent() → authRepository.deliverAuthResponse().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(pro): add sweep engine, analyze command, and profile generation

Phase 3-4 of Ozone Pro diagnostic suite:

sweep.rs — Smart parameter sweep engine:
- Binary search on gpu_layers to find max that fits VRAM budget
- VRAM pruning via planner::estimate_vram_mb() skips OOM configs
- Early stopping when context level exhausts VRAM budget
- Dominated config detection skips redundant benchmarks
- OOM retry with reduced layers
- Pareto frontier tracking with dominance pruning
- Results stored to SQLite automatically

analyze.rs — Benchmark analysis and auto-profiling:
- show_benchmarks: per-model table or all-models summary
- show_pareto: Pareto frontier with profile labels (speed/balanced/context)
- generate_profiles: auto-generates named presets from frontier
- show_profiles: displays stored profiles per-model or globally

main.rs — New subcommands:
- ozone sweep <model> [--max-context N] [--quick]
- ozone analyze [model] [--all] [--generate] [--profiles]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: auto-locate trtexec in common JetPack paths + auto-install

trtexec is not in PATH on most JetPack installs — it lives at
/usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec. Step 10 now:
1. Checks PATH first
2. Searches common JetPack locations
3. Attempts apt-get install tensorrt as fallback
4. Only skips if all methods fail

commit.message:
feat(bench): add ozone bench subcommand

Implements single-model benchmark protocol:
- Clear GPU, launch KoboldCpp, generate, measure, stop
- Measures tokens/sec, TTFT, VRAM peak, RAM peak
- Stores results in SQLite (~/.local/share/ozone/benchmarks.db)
- Uses KoboldCpp /api/extra/perf for accurate token counts
- Handles OOM, timeout, and error cases gracefully

New files:
- src/bench.rs: benchmark protocol (run_benchmark, store_result, print_result)
- src/main.rs: Bench subcommand with --gpu-layers, --context, --quant-kv, --threads

Tested with mn-12b-mag-mell-r1.gguf:
- Full GPU (-1 layers): 48.57 t/s, 8133 MB VRAM
- Mixed (20 layers): 8.15 t/s, 4805 MB VRAM

* feat: internationalize validation errors and error boundaries

- Replaced hardcoded English validation messages in `bookSchema` with translation keys.
- Updated `BookForm` and `book/error.tsx` to dynamically translate error messages using the `useLanguage` hook.
- Added new translation keys for validation errors, demographics description, and generic error states across English, Arabic, and Persian locale files (`en.json`, `ar.json`, `fa.json`).
- Applied a minor formatting adjustment to `AppSidebar.tsx`.

* feat: add i18n support to NotificationDropdown and fix LanguageContext hydration

- Integrated the `useLanguage` hook into `NotificationDropdown` to replace hardcoded text with localized string keys.
- Added corresponding translation keys (`notification`, `requests_permission_to_change`, `project`, `min_ago`, `hr_ago`, `view_all_notifications`, etc.) to English, Arabic, and Persian locale files.
- Resolved a Next.js SSR hydration mismatch in `LanguageContext` by initializing state with the default locale and retrieving the saved locale from `localStorage` within a `useEffect` hook.

* Here is a suggested commit message summarizing the provided diffs:

Introduce multi-language localization and refine error handling

- Added Persian (`fa`) and Arabic (`ar`) resource files for `BookErrors` in the Domain layer.
- Configured request localization middleware in the API project (`Program.cs`) to support English, Persian, and Arabic cultures.
- Converted static readonly fields in `BookErrors.cs` to expression-bodied properties to allow dynamic evaluation of localized resources per request.
- Updated the frontend fetch utility (`orval-fetch.ts`) to automatically inject the `Accept-Language` header based on the user's active locale.
- Enhanced the frontend error handler (`error-handler.ts`) to attempt JSON parsing for `ProblemDetails` when API errors are returned as raw strings.

Fix run-tasks.ps1 parser to match current plan format

The regex was splitting on === lines then looking for 'Task:' and
'Implemented: false' in the same block, but the plan format puts
them in adjacent blocks separated by a === line. Switched to a
single regex that matches across the separator line.

Bump pyrefly from 0.58.0 to 0.60.0 in /backend (#1647) Bumps [pyrefly](https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly) from 0.58.0 to 0.60.0.
Release notes

Sourced from pyrefly's releases.

Pyrefly v0.60.0

Full Changelog: https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly/compare/0.59.1...0.60.0

Pyrefly v0.59.1

Fixed a performance regression in 0.59.0.

Full Changelog: https://github.com/facebook/pyrefly/compare/0.59.0...0.59.1

Pyrefly v0.59.0

Status : Beta
Release date: March 30, 2026

Pyrefly v0.59.0 bundles 153 commits from 20 contributors.

✨ New & Improved

Area What’s new
Type Checking - You can now use while...else statements with returns in the else clause without triggering a false positive missing-explicit-return error. - Pyrefly now correctly handles type inference for nested empty dictionaries when constructing TypedDict instances, avoiding implicit-any errors. - Error messages now highlight related code with inline labels; for example, an unsupported * operation will show the types of both operands directly in the source snippet
Language Server - LSP hover information for classes now displays constructor signature and docstring. - Support additional LSP functionality for notebooks, including find-references and rename.
Performance - Faster typechecking in large pythonc codebases, up to 2x faster on recent benchmarks on real world projects - Reduced CPU usage through smarter caching of module resolution results - Improved performance of the LSP server by reducing redundant workspace diagnostic publishes.

🐛 bug fixes

We closed 16 bug issues this release 👏

  • #2026: Fixed an issue where recursive bounded generics were incorrectly reported as object, ensuring accurate type checking.
  • #2812: Resolved a false positive invalid-type-var error when persisting the get method of a fully-annotated dict.
  • #2804: Fixed an implicit-any false positive that occurred with TypedDict items, improving code readability.
  • #2868: Pyrefly now correctly recognizes while...else statements with returns in the else clause as exhaustive.
  • #2814: Enhanced hover information for datetime.datetime imports to display constructor signatures and docstrings.
  • #2896: Fixed a bad-argument-type error that occurred when using double-underscore arguments.
  • #2893: Pyrefly now correctly handles dict Literal key types as subtypes of str key types.
  • #2865: Resolved an issue where tuple subclasses with overridden __getitem__ were not recognized.
  • #2871: Fixed a false positive error when using isinstance with type | X.
  • And more! #2444, #1270, #2900, #2862, #2853

Thank-you to all our contributors who found these bugs and reported them! Did you know this is one of the most helpful contributions you can make to an open-source project? If you find any bugs in Pyrefly we want to know about them! Please open a bug report issue here

📦 Upgrade

pip install --upgrade pyrefly==0.59.0

... (truncated)

commit.message:
Add App vs App Grafana dashboard

Create a new dashboard for comparing metrics across two different apps,
mirroring the structure of Version vs Version. Uses app_dataset_a and
app_dataset_b template variables instead of a single app_dataset with
version selectors.

Update inject-variables.py to detect dashboards with A/B app variables
and inject both dropdowns with the full app list from config.

commit.message:
feat: complete UI redesign with modern glassmorphism dark theme

- Zinc/neutral dark palette replacing blue-tinted colors
- Glassmorphism cards with backdrop-blur and subtle transparency
- Gradient accents (indigo → violet spectrum)
- Animated status cards with accent bar glow effects
- Sleek sidebar with gradient logo, pill-shaped active nav indicators
- Dashboard: hero gradient orbs, skeleton loading, pulsing status dots
- Instances: modal overlay for add/edit, spinner on test, delete confirmation
- RSS Manager: animated slide-down form, glow toggles, filter chips
- Telegram: split sections with descriptive toggles, animated toasts
- Logs: color-coded badges, skeleton loading, staggered row animations
- Settings forms: gradient toggles, focus rings, success toast animations
- Smooth micro-animations (fadeIn, slideUp, pulseGlow keyframes)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(#196): download FunctionGemma alongside Gemma-4 in onboarding (#199)

* fix(#196): download FunctionGemma alongside Gemma-4 in onboarding

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#196): show retry button when one model errors during parallel download

In the isDownloading branch of the download when block, add a conditional
Retry button that surfaces when uiState.anyError is true. This handles the
mixed state where Gemma-4 enters Error while FunctionGemma is still
Downloading — the ModelDownloadRow already shows the per-model error, but
there was previously no way to trigger a retry until FunctionGemma also
settled.

startDownload(GEMMA_4_E2B) is safe to call here because WorkManager's KEEP
policy leaves an already-downloading or downloaded model untouched, so only
the errored model's work chain is restarted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: kernel-ai-assistant
repository.description: Kernel AI Assistant is a high-performance, local-first intelligent agent designed to bridge the gap between cloud-scale reasoning and total data sovereignty. Built specifically for the Android ecosystem, Kernel AI bypasses the cloud entirely, executing state-of-the-art Gemma-4 E-4B and E-2B models directly on your device's NPU and GPU.
